methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $148M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Nearfield Instruments is a Series C-stage semiconductor company founded by Roland van Vliet and Hamed Sadeghian, having raised $147.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 private companies in Chips & Semiconductors at the same or adjacent stage (SambaNova Systems at $5.0B Series D, Tenstorrent at $2.7B Series D, Positron AI at $1.0B Series B, and DEEPX at $529M Series C), plus 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) with a median price-to-sales multiple of 25.3x.

Two methods proved applicable. Stage-Progression, using the $148M total raised as a proxy valuation and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yields $251M. Peer-Median triangulation across the 4-company set produced $1.9B, reflecting the wide dispersion in the peer universe (range $529M to $5.0B). Public-comp anchoring was not executable due to the absence of Nearfield's revenue figures, common among pre-exit private companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $213M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.1B. This wide band reflects material uncertainty around stage-specific comparables and the heterogeneity of the peer set, which spans seed-stage through Series D. The median estimate is most conservative relative to peer valuations and accounts for Nearfield's stage position between DEEPX ($529M, Series C) and the larger Series D raises.
